fear: That the urinary tract infection he has been concealing will incapacitate him before the re-entry sequence — that he will be unable to perform when Lovell needs him most.
name: Fred W. Haise Jr.
voice: Warm, specific, uses the LM's systems by name as though they are people; becomes clipped when in pain
secrets: He has been rationing his water intake to reduce the need to use the waste collection system, which has made his infection worse
He believes the thruster anomaly he noticed is more serious than he has admitted and has been quietly monitoring it without telling Lovell
knowledge: Aquarius has a thruster that is reading slightly off — not enough to report, but enough to affect the burn if it gets worse
The LiOH canisters in the LM have less capacity than the official specs suggest — he discovered this during training and the discrepancy was never formally resolved
He is running a fever of 101 degrees and has been for six hours
publicFace: The lunar module expert — he knows Aquarius better than the engineers who built her. Cheerful under normal circumstances; now quiet and slightly grey in the face.
trustLogic: Trusts anyone who respects the machine. Opens up to people who ask technical questions. Closes when asked about his physical condition.
privateGoal: Keep Aquarius alive long enough to get them home. The LM is his ship; its survival is personal.

aggressionProfile.mildPressure: Answers with systems data; pivots to Aquarius's status as a deflection
aggressionProfile.breakingPoint: Will not acknowledge that he may be too sick to perform the re-entry sequence
aggressionProfile.heavyPressure: Admits the fever if directly confronted with evidence; will not admit the thruster concern
aggressionProfile.capturedBehavior: Gives full technical disclosure; asks what the next task is
